snippets.title: Lock with pip-tools (pip-compile)
snippets.code:
pip install pip-tools

# requirements.in holds loose intent:
#   openai>=1.40,<2
#   langchain

pip-compile --generate-hashes requirements.in   # -> requirements.txt (pinned + hashes)
pip-sync requirements.txt                        # make the env match exactly
snippets.explanation: requirements.in is the manifest; pip-compile produces a fully pinned, hashed lock; pip-sync enforces it.

snippets.title: A hash-pinned requirements.txt (excerpt)
snippets.code:
openai==1.40.3 \
    --hash=sha256:....
httpx==0.27.0 \
    --hash=sha256:....
# every direct AND transitive package pinned with a verified hash
snippets.explanation: Exact versions plus hashes make installs deterministic and tamper-evident.

snippets.title: Lock-respecting installs per tool
snippets.code:
poetry install                 # uses poetry.lock
pipenv install --deploy         # fails if Pipfile.lock is stale
pip install -r requirements.txt # if requirements.txt is fully pinned
conda-lock install              # cross-platform conda lock
snippets.explanation: Each ecosystem has a command that installs the recorded set rather than re-resolving.
